question: q02
question_title: 小行星采矿未来愿景及公平影响
statement: What might asteroid mining look like in the future, and how might asteroid mining impact global equity?
methods: 比较 private first mover、state-led blocs、UN licensed benefit sharing、open science coalition 四种未来愿景，计算公平、可行性和条约对齐得分。

data_source.root: docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets/2022/All for One and One (Space) for All!
data_source.source_pdf: docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets/2022/All for One and One (Space) for All!

scenarios.description: The UN updates treaty practice through licenses, public reporting, safety rules, and a global benefit fund.
Public agencies, firms, and universities share mission data while firms compete under common benefit obligations.
Several spacefaring states run national programs and form exclusive resource blocs.
A few private firms and sponsor states finance extraction and retain most mineral profits.

recommended_vision.description: The UN updates treaty practice through licenses, public reporting, safety rules, and a global benefit fund.

asteroid_mining_vision.vision_note: The recommended vision balances feasible public-private mining with UN licensing, benefit sharing, data access, and risk bonding.

equity_model.definition: Global equity in asteroid mining means broad benefit sharing, fair access to participation, technology diffusion, multilateral voice, risk protection, and peaceful use.

dimensions.benefit_sharing: Mineral value and derived public benefits reach countries irrespective of economic or scientific development.
dimensions.access_to_participation: Emerging space nations, universities, and public agencies can participate in missions, data, and contracts.
dimensions.technology_transfer: Mission knowledge, safety practices, and enabling infrastructure diffuse beyond first movers.
dimensions.governance_voice: Rules are set through transparent multilateral governance, not only by mining states or firms.
dimensions.risk_and_environmental_burden: Launch, reentry, orbital-debris, market, and terrestrial environmental risks are not shifted to low-power countries.
dimensions.peaceful_use_and_conflict_prevention: Resource extraction remains aligned with the Outer Space Treaty and does not militarize access to space resources.

validation_logic: Outer Space Treaty benchmark: higher scores require benefits for all countries irrespective of development level.
International Space Station and satellite access benchmark: multinational access and shared technical infrastructure improve participation equity.
Exclusive concession benchmark: concentrated finance without benefit sharing lowers equity even when technical feasibility is high.
